title: Avoid Synchronizing on a 'Lock' Object in Java
description: In the Java language, java.util.concurrent.locks.Lock offers more robust and flexible locking operations compared to synchronized blocks. Synchronizing on a Lock instance discards the power of the object, as it supersedes its superior locking mechanisms. Instead, use the object's lock and unlock method variants for locking and unlocking.
recommendation: Replace the use of synchronized blocks on Lock instances with the object's lock and unlock methods.
impact: Using synchronized blocks on Lock instances can result in less efficient and flexible lock operations, thereby potentially affecting the performance and robustness of your Java application.

checks.message: Replace the use of synchronized blocks on Lock instances with the object's lock and unlock methods.
